title: Modify Pointers
description: Change the value of variables through dereferenced pointers.

slides.title: Modifying Pointer Values ✏️
slides.content: Since dereferencing a pointer gives us access to the exact data item living in RAM, we can actually use pointers to securely overwrite that data from anywhere in the app!

slides.title: Remote Destruction
slides.content: Changing the food variable, from miles away.

quizData.question: Why did `cout << food` output Hamburger, even though we never reassigned the `food` variable directly?

options.text: Because `*ptr = "Hamburger";` dug into the memory address housing `food` and directly modified the contents.
Because `ptr` is a faster variable type than `string`

quizData.explanation: By dereferencing the pointer, you have physical control of the original memory location.

slides.title: Why Pointers? 🔑
slides.content:
Like references, pointers allow developers to grant other functions remote access to gigabytes of data locked in one spot, saving memory overhead.

They also allow for complex data constructs like Linked Lists and Trees!

quizData.question: Assigning `ptr = "Hamburger";` (without the `*`) would properly overwrite the string.
quizData.explanation: Since `ptr` stores a memory address, not text, attempting to shove a string `"Hamburger"` directly into the address block is invalid! You MUST dereference it with `*` first.
